name: Telluride Association Summer Program — EAST

cost.notes: COMPLETELY FREE — tuition, room, and board all covered

essays.prompt: Read the assigned reading and write a critical response. Discuss your intellectual interests and how they connect to technology and society.
tips: EAST focuses on the relationship between technology and society — show you have thought carefully about this

outcomes.college_impact: Same strong signal as TASP Humanities — Telluride Association programs are uniformly prestigious.
outcomes.typical_results: Similar to TASP Humanities — Telluride alumni attend top Ivy League and liberal arts colleges.

what_they_look_for: Intellectual curiosity about the intersection of technology and society
Critical thinking about technology's role in social change

blurbs.short: A free 6-week interdisciplinary seminar focused on technology and society, run by the Telluride Association.
blurbs.long: TASP EAST (East Asian Studies/Topics variant) is one of several programs run by the Telluride Association. Like all TASPs, EAST is completely free and selects 24 students per session for an intensive seminar experience. The EAST seminar focuses on East Asian studies, international relations, or technology and society topics (varies by year and location). Students read challenging texts, write extensively, and drive seminar discussions. The Telluride Association is famous for its rigorous academic culture and its commitment to socioeconomic diversity — the free tuition is a core part of the mission, not an afterthought. The application is equally demanding across all TASP variants.

curriculum: Seminar discussions, independent reading, essay writing, collaborative projects.
